title: onobrychis iberica
intro: Onobrychis iberica, also known as Persian clover or Iberian broom, is a plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Caucasus, Turkey, and the Middle East. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing blood sugar levels, lowering cholesterol levels, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Onobrychis iberica is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
